copy.caption: The white whale that can make faces — and sounds like a busy playground.
copy.shortDescription: Beluga whales are nicknamed sea canaries because they make so many chirps, squeaks, clicks, and whistles that you can sometimes hear them right through the hull of a boat. Unlike most whales, belugas have a flexible neck and can turn their head sideways — and they can change the shape of their forehead bubble to help make different sounds. Fun fact: Belugas are one of the few whales that can swim backward.

photo.attributionName: Javier Yaya Tur / CIUDAD DE LAS ARTES Y LAS CIENCIAS from Valencia, España
